prompt:
The dock intake feed replays badge scans, so every downstream report first pastes the same ten lines of dedupe boilerplate. I want that pulled into one real pipeline function, not another helper that slurps an array. Ship `pipetally.ps1` in the repo root, dot-sourceable, defining `Select-UniqueRecord`.

Requirements, and I care about the plumbing as much as the behavior:

- It must be a proper advanced function with begin/process/end blocks that STREAMS. Our progress logger sits downstream in the same pipeline, and today it goes silent for the whole run and then spews everything at the end when someone writes a collect-then-emit function. The tests pin the interleaving: each first-seen record must reach the next pipeline stage before the following input record is read.
- Input comes either from the pipeline (`$scans | Select-UniqueRecord`) or as an argument (`Select-UniqueRecord -InputObject $scans`). Both shapes must behave IDENTICALLY — same records, same order, same summary. People forget that a parameter-bound array arrives in one lump; handle it.
- Dedupe key: `-Key <propertyName>`, default `Id`. First record for a key wins and is emitted as-is (the original object, not a copy with reordered properties); later records with the same key are dropped but still counted. Key values are compared as strings, case-SENSITIVELY (`srv-A` and `srv-a` are different assets), and stringification means an integer 7 and a string '7' collide — that's fine, our tag printers do the same thing.
- `$null` items in the feed are skipped entirely: never emitted, never counted, no error.
- A record that doesn't have the key property at all is a feed bug: throw with message exactly `pipetally: record is missing key property 'Id'` (whatever the key name is). Careful: the tests run everything under Set-StrictMode Latest, so probe for the property properly instead of just reading it and hoping.
- `-Summary` switch: emit NO records while streaming; when the input is drained, emit one `[pscustomobject]` per distinct key, in first-seen order, with properties in exactly this order: `Key` (the stringified key) then `Count` (Int32 number of times the key appeared, duplicates included).
- Empty input (either shape, either mode) emits nothing.

Acceptance: `pwsh -NoProfile -File test_pipetally.ps1` from the workspace root must pass unmodified — don't touch the test file.
notes: 6P3 item 1: proper begin/process/end pipeline function, streaming dedupe+count, pipeline and -InputObject pinned identical. PowerShell-native pins with no bash analog: process-block streaming proven by an interleave log (feed:/emit: alternation — a collect-then-emit implementation fails), [Parameter(ValueFromPipeline)] parameter binding where the argument shape arrives as one array in a single process call (implementation must iterate), begin/end state for the -Summary property-bag emission, StrictMode-safe property probing via PSObject.Properties. Differentiation vs bash-inventory (named sibling): that seed is associative-array bookkeeping over text lines; here the entire contract is object-pipeline semantics (streaming order, property bags, binding shapes) which bash cannot express. Probed on this box 2026-07-16: piped input wraps each item into a fresh [object[]] per process call while -InputObject binds the whole array once; HashSet/Dictionary[string] default comparer is ordinal case-sensitive; interleave log for the reference is exactly feed:a emit:a feed:b emit:b feed:a feed:c emit:c.
